title: हनुमान हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान हृदयम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
वज्रदेहं महावीरं दैत्यदर्पनिषूदनम्।
सीताशोकविनाशार्थं रामदूतं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ हृदयम् ॥
नमो हनुमते तुभ्यं नमो मारुतसूनवे।
नमः श्रीरामदूताय कपिवीराय ते नमः॥ १॥

त्वमेव मातापिता त्वमेव त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव।
त्वमेव विद्या द्रविणं त्वमेव त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवदेव॥ २॥

प्राणोऽसि रामस्य महाबलस्त्वं
लङ्काविदाही रिपुसैन्यनाशी।
सीताविशोकं विदधासि वीर
नमामि नित्यं पवनात्मजं त्वाम्॥ ३॥

त्वं ज्ञानगम्यं परमं निधानं
त्वं वायुपुत्रोऽसि महानुभावः।
त्वं ब्रह्मचारी बलवीर्यरूपं
त्वं सेवितोऽसि प्रभुरामचन्द्रे॥ ४॥

आञ्जनेय महावीर मनःसंस्मरणं हरे।
सर्वरोगहरं चैव सर्वसम्पत्तिकारणम्॥ ५॥

यत्र यत्र रघुनाथकीर्तनं तत्र तत्र कृतमस्तकाञ्जलिम्।
बाष्पवारिपरिपूर्णलोचनं मारुतिं नमत राक्षसान्तकम्॥ ६॥

बुद्धिर्बलं यशो धैर्यं निर्भयत्वमरोगता।
अजाड्यं वाक्पटुत्वं च हनूमत्स्मरणाद् भवेत्॥ ७॥

सर्वदुःखहरं देवं सर्वमङ्गलकारणम्।
हनूमद्धृदयं पुण्यं ये पठन्ति दिने दिने॥ ८॥

ते लभन्ते महाभाग्यं श्रीरामस्य प्रसादतः।
सर्वपापहरं पुण्यं सर्वशत्रुविनाशनम्॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री हनुमान हृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Hridayam ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Vajradeham Mahaveeram Daityadarpanishudanam.
Sitashokvinashartham Ramadutam Namamyaham.

|| Hridayam ||
Namo Hanumate Tubhyam Namo Marutasuunave.
Namah Shri Ramadutaya Kapiveeraya Te Namah. || 1 ||

Tvameva Matapita Tvameva Tvameva Bandhushcha Sakha Tvameva.
Tvameva Vidya Dravinam Tvameva Tvameva Sarvam Mama Devadeva. || 2 ||

Prano'si Ramasya Mahabalstvam
Lankavidahi Ripusainyanashi.
Sitavishokam Vidadhasi Veera
Namami Nityam Pavanatmajam Tvam. || 3 ||

Tvam Jnanagamyam Paramam Nidhanam
Tvam Vayuputro'si Mahanubhavah.
Tvam Brahmachari Balaveerya-rupam
Tvam Sevito'si Prabhu-Ramachandra. || 4 ||

Anjaneya Mahaveera Manahsamsmaranam Hare.
Sarvarogaharam Chaiva Sarvasampattikaranam. || 5 ||

Yatra Yatra Raghunathakiratanam Tatra Tatra Kritamastakanjlim.
Bashpavaariparipurnalochanam Marutim Namata Rakshsaantakam. || 6 ||

Buddhirbalam Yasho Dhairyam Nirbhayatvamarogata.
Ajaadyam Vaakpatuttvam Cha Hanumatsmaranaat Bhavet. || 7 ||

Sarvadukhaharam Devam Sarvamangalakaranam.
Hanumad-hridayam Punyam Ye Pathanti Dine Dine. || 8 ||

Te Labhante Mahabhagyam Shri Ramasya Prasadatah.
Sarvapapaharam Punyam Sarvashatruvinashanam. || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Hanuman Hridayam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: यह स्तोत्र हनुमान जी के हृदय (सार तत्व) का वर्णन करता है। हनुमान जी वज्र-शरीर, महावीर, दैत्यों के दर्प को नष्ट करने वाले और श्रीराम के परम दूत हैं। वे माता-पिता, बन्धु-सखा, विद्या और धन सब कुछ हैं। वे लंका को जलाने वाले, शत्रु-सेना का नाश करने वाले और सीता माता के शोक को दूर करने वाले हैं। हनुमान जी के स्मरण मात्र से बुद्धि, बल, यश, धैर्य, निर्भयता, आरोग्य और वाक्पटुता प्राप्त होती है। जो प्रतिदिन इस हृदय स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है उसे महाभाग्य और श्रीराम जी की कृपा प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of courage, strength, and fearlessness
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and grants perfect health
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and obstacles in life
Invokes wisdom, eloquence, and sharp intellect
Traditionally associated with the complete grace and blessings of lord rama
description: The Hanuman Hridayam, known in Sanskrit as हनुमान हृदयम्, is a heart-essence prayer in praise of Lord Hanuman. This sacred composition is sourced from Hanuman Samhita and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bring the complete grace and blessings of Lord Rama as well as remove all diseases and grant perfect health. Additionally, it is known to grant wisdom, eloquence, and sharp intellect. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti. Comprising 9 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Sacred hymn to Lord Hanuman for courage, health, and divine protection.
relatedSearches: Hanuman prayers for fear and obstacles

seoFaqs.question: What does the term 'Hridayam' mean in stotra titles like Hanuman Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Hridaya literally means heart, but in stotra titles it carries the technical sense of essence, core, or innermost teaching. A 'Hridaya' text is therefore the heart-essence prayer of a deity, distilling the most powerful invocations into a compact form. The classical model is the Aditya Hridayam in the Yuddha Kanda of the Ramayana, which Sage Agastya teaches Rama as the heart-prayer of the Sun. Hanuman Hridayam follows the same naming convention: a concentrated prayer addressed to Hanuman that is meant to carry the full weight of his protective grace in a short, repeatable form suited for daily practice.

seoFaqs.question: From which scriptural source is Hanuman Hridayam traditionally drawn?
seoFaqs.answer: Several Hanuman 'hridaya' and protective stotras circulate within the Sudarshan Samhita lineage, a Vaishnava-tantric collection that also preserves the well-known Vibhishana-krit Hanuman Stotra. These texts present themselves as dialogues in which devotees such as Vibhishana praise Hanuman to obtain protection against fear, illness, and evil influences. Different printed pothi editions group the verses under slightly different titles; the same body of verses may appear as 'Hanuman Hridaya Stotra', 'Hanumat Hridayam' or as part of a longer Hanuman kavacha. Devotees should ideally rely on a printed edition with parallel Sanskrit and translation rather than ad-hoc internet copies.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do traditional sources attribute to Hanuman Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Texts in the Sudarshan Samhita lineage describe Hanuman Hridayam as effective in removing all kinds of fears (bhaya), obstacles (vighna), illnesses (roga), enemy troubles (shatru-badha), and influences attributed to malevolent forces (preta-pishacha). Practitioners report psychological benefits as well: reduced anxiety, increased courage when facing professional or social difficulties, and a sense of protective presence during travel. The texts attribute these effects to Hanuman's identity as Rudra-amsa (a portion of Shiva) and his role as the eternal servant of Rama, which gives his name an ability to dispel both inner fears and outer troubles.

seoFaqs.question: When and how is Hanuman Hridayam ideally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesdays and Saturdays are the traditional Hanuman days, and both dawn and dusk are auspicious times. A simple practice: bathe, wear clean clothes (red or saffron is favoured), sit facing east, light a sesame-oil lamp before an image of Hanuman, offer red flowers and a small naivedya (often jaggery and gram or boondi), and recite the stotram once or in cycles of 11, 21 or 108. Many devotees pair it with the Hanuman Chalisa and a few rounds of the Hanuman moola mantra. Continuity over weeks matters more than the count on any single day. Women, children and elders may all recite it without restriction.

seoFaqs.question: How is Hanuman Hridayam different from the more familiar Hanuman Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Chalisa, composed by Goswami Tulsidas in Awadhi, is a forty-verse popular devotional hymn that has spread across the Hindi-speaking world and beyond. Hanuman Hridayam, by contrast, is a Sanskrit stotra preserved within the Sudarshan Samhita lineage and is shorter, more mantric in feel, and focused specifically on the protective heart-essence aspect of Hanuman. The Chalisa is broader in scope, narrating Hanuman's biography and exhorting general devotion; the Hridayam is tighter, functioning more like an armoured prayer for protection and removal of obstacles. Many devotees recite both: the Chalisa for emotional connection and the Hridayam as a focused protective practice.
